content:
Key Findings

  • Preston City Council spent £205.9M across 7 financial years
  • The top 5 suppliers received £82.2M, accounting for 39.9% of total spend
  • MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS was the largest single supplier, receiving £31.5M (15.3% of total spend)
  • The top 10 suppliers combined received £100.9M (49.0% of total spend)
  • The remaining 2,507 suppliers shared £105.0M (51.0% of total spend)

Preston's Biggest Contracts

Preston City Council spent £205.9M across 7 financial years, with a significant portion going to a small number of suppliers. The top 5 suppliers alone received £82.2M, which is 39.9% of the total spend. This concentration of spending raises questions about value for money and the effectiveness of the council's procurement strategy.

The largest single supplier was MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS, which received £31.5M (15.3% of total spend). This substantial amount suggests that MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS may be providing critical services or infrastructure projects for the council. However, the high percentage of total spend directed to a single supplier warrants scrutiny to ensure that taxpayer money is being used efficiently.

Top Suppliers and Their Roles

The top 10 suppliers combined received £100.9M (49.0% of total spend), leaving the remaining 2,507 suppliers to share £105.0M (51.0%). This distribution indicates that a small number of suppliers are playing a significant role in the council's operations. Among the top suppliers, LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL received £28.0M (13.6% of total spend), suggesting a high level of inter-council collaboration or service provision.

CONLON CONSTRUCTION LTD received £10.6M (5.2% of total spend), indicating a substantial investment in construction projects. Other notable suppliers include ERIC WRIGHT CIVIL ENGINEERING LTD with £6.7M (3.2%) and ZURICH MUNICIPAL with £5.3M (2.6%). These figures highlight the council's focus on infrastructure and engineering services.

Value for Money Analysis

To determine whether these high-value contracts represent good value for taxpayers, it is essential to examine the outcomes and benefits delivered by these suppliers. For instance, MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS and CONLON CONSTRUCTION LTD may be involved in critical infrastructure projects that benefit the community. However, without detailed performance metrics and transparency, it is challenging to assess the true value of these contracts.

The concentration of spending among a few suppliers also raises questions about competition and market diversity. A diverse supplier base can drive innovation and ensure that the council gets the best value for its money. The council should regularly review its procurement strategy to ensure that it is fostering a competitive market and achieving the best possible outcomes for taxpayers.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council about the specific projects and services provided by MAPLE GROVE DEVELOPMENTS and CONLON CONSTRUCTION LTD
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about the council's procurement processes and spending decisions
  • Check the council's published contracts register to review the terms and conditions of high-value contracts
  • Write to your local councillor about the concentration of spending among a few suppliers and ask for transparency on the value delivered by these contracts
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends in Preston City Council's procurement
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
